a generic electron transport chain
NADH is oxidized to NAD +
protein in chain takes electrons, is reduced
electrons move to lower energy level
with each step
Oxygen is “terminal electron acceptor”
½ O 2 + 2e - + 2 H + = H 2 O

The real thing -
don’t learn names for proteins
note energy level of electrons

XXXX

Electron Transport Chain – proteins in inner mitochondrial membrane
energy from electrons used to power proton pumps (active transport!)

Electrochemical gradient across inner mitochondrial membrane
more H + , more + charge in intermembrane space than in matrix

ATP synthase complex
in inner mitochondrial membrane
allows H + to re-enter matrix.
move down electrochemical gradient
it spins!

Proton motive force
potential energy

kinetic energy

chemical energy
(bond in ATP)

oxidative phosphorylation
energy to power
ADP + P  ATP
comes from redox reactions
proton pumps, H + gradient

be able to account for the ATP
know where NADH and FADH 2 are reduced and oxidized
